content.value: Docker began as an internal tool at dotCloud, a PaaS company founded by Solomon Hykes. After its public debut in March 2013, it quickly became the industry standard for containerization, leading the company to rebrand as Docker, Inc.

items: 2015: Open Container Initiative (OCI) is formed to create industry standards.
2017: The Moby Project is launched to modularize the Docker platform.

content.value: To support the growing ecosystem and tools like Kubernetes, Docker evolved from a single monolithic binary into a set of specialized, modular components: containerd (lifecycle management), runc (the low-level runtime), and the Docker Engine (high-level API).

content.caption: The Evolution of Docker: From dotCloud to Modern Modular Infrastructure

content.value: The Open Container Initiative (OCI) ensures that containers remain portable across different platforms by defining standards for both the **Runtime** (how containers run) and the **Image Format** (how they are packaged).
